title: Level 206
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 206 - Unconvention}} {{VoidedTheme}} {{Blurbackground|file=206-1.jpg}} {{RecommendedAudioNest|audio={{Audio|round=yes|file=All you are going to want to do is get back there .mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.5}}{{Audio|round=yes|file=Level0LightAmbience.mp3|align=center|loop=true|volume=0.5}}|text=enable page ambience for best experience:}} {{Class Custom Image|bg-image=206-2.jpg|hexagon=2|class=2|safety=Unsafe|security=Enviromental Hazards|entity=Minor Hostile Prescence}}
Level 206 is the 207th level of The Backrooms, discovered on May 16th 2022 by [[Blackwood]] of the M.E.G during an expedition into [[Level 4]] == Description == {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=206-1.jpg|text=A photograph of Level 206.}} Level 206 is an open expanse of convention halls and rooms that seem to lack any structuring of a convention itself, with stalls being obsolete within the level. The level's walls and infrastructure are covered with white or beige paint that is constantly liquid, never drying, even when intense heat is applied to the surface. This constant liquidation allows fungi to grow within the concrete on rare occasions, causing structural instability and health problems if the spores are ingested. This paint also induces severe hives and rashes in any wanderers who come into contact with it, along with increased sweating in the feet. While not lethal, it is not recommended to touch the paint. The floor in Level 206 is covered in a thick layer of wet carpet, with the design varying in certain areas. This carpet is known to constantly emit a foul odor that is similar to sweat and pickle juice mixed. This smell causes mild nausea and headaches in any wanderers who inhale it; as such, air filtration devices are recommended when exploring Level 206. The carpet also makes a squelching sound, similar to wet mud, when stepped on. This sound is known to cause severe discomfort to anything that can hear it; whatever causes it is currently being researched. Still, one theory suggests that the carpet fibers contain massive amounts of microscopic dirt invisible to the human eye. The more open, flat areas in Level 206 are illuminated by suspended spotlights supported by steel framing. These spotlights constantly project light at varying intensities and hues; some may be broken or turned off indefinitely. This phenomenon is especially common in the farther regions of Level 206's confines due to the lack of electricity. Some of these spotlights may suddenly fall off the steel framing and noclip into another level, most commonly [[Level 296]] due to its proximity to this level; other reports suggest uncommon appearances in [[Level 114]] and [[Level 298]]. These occurrences have been the cause of multiple deaths within the level, most being due to the blunt force trauma that these falling spotlights inflict on the human body, specifically to the cranium and ribcage. {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=206-2.jpg|text=One of the many hallways in Level 206.}} Multiple articles of clothing can be found strewn about within Level 206, specifically recently worn fursuits, although many other pieces may be found as well. These fursuits appear to be heavily damaged or covered in black mold and stink of sweat. Instances of [[Entity 72]] may also hide within these fursuits; as such, it is not advised to investigate any of them, for the reasons stated earlier. The fursuits may differ from each other or be exact copies of one another, usually a copy of another fursuit within the room if there is one on the premises. The level's entities may also wear these fursuits to deceive wanderers and lure them into dangerous areas within Level 206 as well. Tables, chairs, and other furniture items may spawn randomly around Level 206's premises. These furniture items appear to be more pristine than any other items found within the level, with their materials in the finest condition and the furniture smelling of fresh lavender; over time, this scent will fade. The furniture exhibits an interesting phenomenon: any intentional damage instantly triggers it to repair itself, regardless of how severe the damage was. The contents on the tables include rotten fruit or desserts, plant vases, and promotional leaflets for the non-existent booths on the level. Any attempt to pick up these items will lead to the item vanishing entirely, along with any entities being alerted to the wanderer's location. Music that plays from an unknown source can be heard throughout the level; it is often similar to mall music from the frontrooms, though a crackling noise overlays it at all times, and the volume may change in a few areas. Every three days in the level, the music will periodically stop and transition into another song, during this period the lights will turn off until the level is pitch black, after the music resumes every object within a twenty mile radius of wherever the wanderer is will shuffle positions and any fursuit within the area wil begin twitching, along with their eye sockets turning black with a white ring shaped pupil in the center. This event is theorized to be an instance of [[Phenomenon 12-A]] that is unique to Level 206 itself, as no other levels appear to experience this phenomenon {{User:Liminalityyyyy/Template:ClassicImageBox|file=206-3.jpg|text=An exit to [[Level 296]].}} == Entities == Occasional reports suggest [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 10|Skin Stealers]], and [[Entity 72|Foams]] can be found within Level 206 in uncommon occurrences. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to Level 206 being affected by [[Phenomenon 15]], colonies are unable to be established. == Entrances and Exits == ===Entrances=== *White hallways in [[Level 4|The Abandoned Offices]] lead into Level 206. *The [[Level 5|Terror Hotel]] may transition into the hallway areas at random. *Unmarked stores within [[Level 195|The Yule's Festival Of Dusk]] can lead into the convention rooms of Level 206 *The Red, Blue and Purple Lines in [[The Metro]] have stops that can exit into the open areas of Level 206 ===Exits=== *A pair of glass sliding doors that can be found in the hallways leads into [[Level 298|Watering Down]] *Some areas have brown doors that leads into either [[Level 19|Abyss Inn]] or [[Level 959|somewhere you don't want to know]] *Escalators can grant entry into [[Level 296|Vacancy]] *Passing through multiple doors in a short span of time leads to [[Level 189|The Desolate Resort]] {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:Sammoyoyoyo|Sammy]] '''BGM''': All you are going to want to do is go back there by The Caretaker {{User:Sammoyoyoyo/Pagelist}} !!PLEASE DO NOT ADD ANYTHING EXCEPT GRAMMAR FIXES AND ENTRANCES/EXITS WITHOUT MY PERMISSION OR I WILL REQUEST THE PAGE TO BE LOCKED!!}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Darkness]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Environmental Hazards]]
